---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

Compare different read cut-offs for eDNA samples
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(llnl)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)
library(patchwork)
```


# Alpha diversity metrics
```{r}
alpha.fun <- function(df){
  df_ <- t(df)
  data.frame(
  SampleID = rownames(df_),
  Richness = rowSums(df_ > 0),
  Shannon = diversity(df_, index = "shannon"),
  Simpson = diversity(df_, index = "simpson"),
  InvSimpson = diversity(df_, index = "invsimpson")
) %>% 
  mutate(Evenness = Shannon / log(Richness),
         treatment = case_when(
           grepl("T0",SampleID) ~ "Dry",
           grepl("P",SampleID) ~ "Phosphorus",
           TRUE ~ "No Phosphorus"
         ),
         time.point = substr(SampleID,1,2))
}
```

# Data
```{r}
one_read <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_table1.csv", row.names = 1)

three_read <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna_normalized_table.csv", row.names = 1)

three_read_sample <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_table_3reads_sample.csv", row.names = 1)

ten_read <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_table10.csv", row.names = 1)

hundred_read <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_table100.csv", row.names = 1)

traditional <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_trad.csv", row.names = 1) # Filtering with 3 reads did not make a difference so did not include in analysis

cov10  <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_trad_10_cov.csv", row.names = 1)

cov10_3reads <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_trad_10_cov_3reads.csv", row.names = 1)

cov_5 <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_trad_5_cov.csv", row.names = 1)

cov_5_3reads <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/edna/edna_normalized_trad_5_cov_3reads.csv", row.names = 1)

datasets <- list(
  "1 read"      = one_read,
  "3 reads"     = three_read,
  "3 reads\nper sample" = three_read_sample,
  "10 reads"    = ten_read,
  "100 reads"   = hundred_read,
  "5% Breadth" = cov_5, 
  "5% Breadth +\n>3 reads" = cov_5_3reads, 
  "10% Breadth" = cov10,
  "10% Breadth +\n>3 reads" = cov10_3reads,
  "Traditional" = traditional
)
```

# Compare the number of vOTUs that are present in each table
```{r}
total_votus <- tibble(
  Cutoff = factor(names(datasets), levels = names(datasets)),
  Richness = sapply(datasets, nrow)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Filter_Type = case_when(
      Cutoff %in% c("1 read", "3 reads", 
                    "10 reads", "100 reads")             ~ "Pooled Read cutoff",
      Cutoff %in% c("5% Breadth", "10% Breadth", "Traditional") ~ "Breadth cutoff",
      Cutoff %in% c("5% Breadth +\n>3 reads", 
                    "10% Breadth +\n>3 reads")           ~ "Pooled read + Breadth cutoff",
      Cutoff %in% c("3 reads\nper sample")           ~ "Per sample cutoff",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

filtering_richness <- ggplot(total_votus, aes(x = Cutoff, y = Richness, fill = Filter_Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Richness), vjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Pooled Read cutoff"           = "#2c7fb8",
    "Per sample cutoff"     = "#41ab5d",  
    "Breadth cutoff"        = "#d95f0e",
    "Pooled read + Breadth cutoff" = "#7570b3"    
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
  ) +
  labs(title = "vOTUs retained at different filtering thresholds",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Number of vOTUs",
       fill = "Filter type")

print(filtering_richness)
```

# Compare the number of vOTUs that are present in each sample across the different tables
```{r}
alpha_combined <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(datasets), function(label) {
    alpha.fun(datasets[[label]]) %>% mutate(Cutoff = label)
  })
) %>%
  mutate(Cutoff = factor(Cutoff, levels = names(datasets)))

# Add a Filter_Group column to alpha_combined
alpha_combined <- alpha_combined %>%
  mutate(
    Filter_Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Cutoff, "Breadth") | Cutoff == "Traditional" ~ "Breadth-based filters",
      TRUE                                                     ~ "Read-based filters"
    ),
    Filter_Group = factor(Filter_Group, 
                          levels = c("Read-based filters", "Breadth-based filters"))
  )

# See if trend stat true for richness (T0>T2>T1>T3)
all.time.kruskal.test <- alpha_combined %>% 
  group_by(Cutoff) %>%
  rstatix::kruskal_test(Richness ~ time.point)

all.time.kruskal.test.sig <- all.time.kruskal.test %>% 
  filter(p < 0.05)

all.dunn.time <- alpha_combined %>% 
  filter(Cutoff %in% all.time.kruskal.test.sig$Cutoff) %>%
  mutate(Cutoff = factor(Cutoff, levels = unique(alpha_combined$Cutoff))) %>% # preserve full level order
  group_by(Cutoff) %>%
  rstatix::dunn_test(Richness ~ time.point) %>% 
  rstatix::add_xy_position() # match dodge.width in plot

# Do the same for the Dunn's test results so they facet correctly
all.dunn.time <- all.dunn.time %>%
  mutate(
    Filter_Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Cutoff, "Breadth") | Cutoff == "Traditional" ~ "Breadth-based filters",
      TRUE                                                     ~ "Read-based filters"
    ),
    Filter_Group = factor(Filter_Group, 
                          levels = c("Read-based filters", "Breadth-based filters"))
  )

cutoff_trends <- ggplot(alpha_combined, aes(x = time.point, y = Richness, color = time.point)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75)) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested_wrap(Filter_Group ~ Cutoff, nrow = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = col.lnll, name = "Time point") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Richness at Different Read Cut-offs", 
       x = "Time point", 
       y = "Richness") +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(all.dunn.time, label = "p.adj.signif", 
                              tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = TRUE,size = 6)

# Get the avg of each time point for each cutoff
alpha_combined %>% 
  group_by(Cutoff, time.point) %>% 
  summarise(mean_richness = mean(Richness))

```

# Number of samples each vOTU is in per cutoff

```{r}
# For each cutoff, count how many samples each vOTU appears in
votu_occurrence <- map_dfr(datasets, function(df) {
  tibble(
    vOTU = rownames(df),
    n_samples_detected = rowSums(df > 0)
  )
}, .id = "Cutoff") %>%
  mutate(Cutoff = factor(Cutoff, levels = names(datasets)))

votu_occurrence_binned <- votu_occurrence %>%
  mutate(
    prevalence_bin = cut(
      n_samples_detected,
      breaks = c(0, 1, 5, 10, 25, 48),  # adjust to your sample count
      labels = c("1", "2-5", "6-10", "11-25", "26-48"),
      include.lowest = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
  count(Cutoff, prevalence_bin)

votu_occurrence_fig <- ggplot(votu_occurrence_binned, aes(x = Cutoff, y = n, fill = prevalence_bin)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Detected in\nN samples") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Number of vOTUs",
       title = "vOTU prevalence distribution across filters")


```

# Bring it all together
```{r}
#Remove the titles from the figures first
cutoff_trends <- cutoff_trends +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

filtering_richness <- filtering_richness +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

votu_occurrence_fig <- votu_occurrence_fig +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

  
combine.fig <- cutoff_trends +(filtering_richness/votu_occurrence_fig) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")

ggsave(plot = combine.fig, "../figures/supplementary_fig1.png", width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300)
```
